data.title: As a downstream client, I am pinned to a stable release tag rather than to main HEAD
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Run scripts/release.sh to perform the cutover: bump plugin.json version to 1.13.0 (if not already), create annotated git tag v1.13.0 on a designated commit on main, edit marketplace.json to re-pin the dso entry's source.ref from main to v1.13.0, commit, and push. After this commit lands, downstream clients running /plugin marketplace update see their installed dso re-resolved to the v1.13.0 tag content.

**Why**: This is the cutover commit — the moment the two-channel model takes effect for existing consumers. Until this story lands, both dso and dso-dev resolve to main HEAD; after it lands, dso is frozen at v1.13.0 until the next release.

**Scope**:
- IN: First execution of scripts/release.sh; creation of v1.13.0 tag; marketplace.json edit re-pinning dso's source.ref to v1.13.0; the resulting commit and push to main.
- OUT: Future releases (this story is the one-shot first cutover); release.sh implementation (Story 2 prerequisite); marketplace.json structure (Story 1 prerequisite); doc updates beyond what release.sh writes (Story 4).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, git tag v1.13.0 exists on origin and the plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json content at that tag declares version: 1.13.0.
  ← Satisfies: \"The first stable release tag (v1.13.0, the next minor bump from the current 1.12.32) is cut from main during epic execution, and the dso entry in marketplace.json is pinned to it via git-subdir\"
- When this story is complete, the dso entry in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json on main has source.source=git-subdir and source.ref=v1.13.0 (no longer ref=main).
  ← Satisfies: \"The first stable release tag (v1.13.0, the next minor bump from the current 1.12.32) is cut from main during epic execution, and the dso entry in marketplace.json is pinned to it via git-subdir\"
- When this story is complete, a downstream client running /plugin marketplace update after the cutover commit sees their installed dso re-resolved to v1.13.0 (not main HEAD).
  ← Satisfies: \"After the cutover commit, a downstream client running /plugin marketplace update sees their installed dso plugin re-resolved to the latest release tag (not to main HEAD)\"
- When this story is complete, a subsequent merge to main (no-op or routine) does not change the content that dso consumers see on their next marketplace refresh — only dso-dev consumers move forward.
  ← Satisfies: \"A merge to main does not change the content that dso consumers see on their next marketplace refresh — only dso-dev consumers move forward\"
- When this story is complete, integration test or manual verification recorded confirming the cutover behavior on a sandbox client.

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Atomic cutover — tag and marketplace.json edit must land in the same push to avoid the window where marketplace.json references a non-existent tag.
- [Reliability] Auto-update OFF clients (the third-party default) won't observe the cutover until they manually /plugin marketplace update — accepted limitation per epic SC6.
- [Security] This story performs the actual push to main — the maintainer running it must explicitly confirm.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
